notes: Aggregate statistics from academic papers; no GTFS-extractable individual routes found. Data points are network-level statistics useful for Wave 2 validation and Wave 3 frequency/length estimation.

network_statistics.puj_ridership_concentration: 65% of passengers on 20% of routes; top 3% of routes carry 20% of passengers

example_routes.frequency_notes: Intra-CBD short route; likely high frequency
example_routes.notes: Cited as shortest PUJ route example (~1 km) in EASTS 2015 passenger demand study. Route name unverified against LTFRB records.

example_routes.frequency_notes: Long-distance PUJ; crosses from Rizal province into NCR fringe
example_routes.notes: Cited as longest PUJ route example (~47 km) in EASTS 2015 passenger demand study. This route extends beyond NCR into Rizal province. Route name unverified against LTFRB records; may be a P2P or provincial route misclassified as PUJ.

sources.paper: Some Observations of PUV Routes in Metro Manila Based on LTFRB Records
sources.key_data: 820 PUJ routes, 60 PUB routes from LTFRB May 2006 franchise records

sources.paper: Understanding Passenger Demand Across Metro Manila
sources.key_data: 677 PUJ routes, 82 PUB routes; PUJ avg 10.9km; 8.96M daily riders; 7,347km total network
55,000 jeepneys, 5,000 buses, 6,483 UV Express in Greater Capital Region; COVID decline stats

sources.paper: Reforms in Metro Manila's Bus Transport System Hastened by COVID-19
sources.key_data: 31 bus routes created during GCQ; EDSA Carousel = Route E; grew to 68 routes by Nov 2024

sources.paper: A Study of Metro Manila's Public Transportation Sector
sources.key_data: LTFRB had ~1,100 bus route records and 516 jeepney Excel files (landmark-based) in 2013 PTIS dataset
